title: Assessing BCE’s Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness And Short Term Recovery
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

What BCE’s recent share performance suggests for investors

BCE (TSX:BCE) has drawn renewed interest after a period where the share price declined about 6% over the past month but showed a modest positive return over the past 3 months.

See our latest analysis for BCE.

At the current share price of CA$33.17, BCE’s recent 1 day and 7 day share price declines sit alongside a positive 90 day share price return and a 12 month total shareholder return of 17.53%. However, longer term total shareholder returns over 3 and 5 years remain negative, which suggests short term momentum has improved even as longer term holders are still under water.

If BCE’s recent moves have you thinking about where else income and defensiveness might come from, it could be worth scanning 6 dividend fortresses

With BCE trading at CA$33.17, sitting at a reported 60% discount to one intrinsic value estimate and below an average analyst price target of CA$37.50, is this a genuine value opportunity, or are markets already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 11.6% Undervalued

Against BCE’s last close of CA$33.17, the most followed narrative pegs fair value at about CA$37.53, using a detailed discounted cash flow approach built on analyst forecasts and a 7.55% discount rate.

Momentum in BCE's AI-powered enterprise solutions (Ateko, cybersecurity, and Bell AI Fabric) is opening up new high-margin business lines. These solutions benefit from the proliferation of AI workloads and digital transformation among Canadian enterprises, with positive implications for consolidated revenues and long-term EBITDA growth.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what justifies that fair value gap? The narrative leans on modest revenue growth, thinner margins, and a future earnings multiple that is higher than today. The exact mix of those inputs is where it gets interesting.

Result: Fair Value of CA$37.53 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this hinges on BCE managing price war pressure in Canadian wireless, as well as the heavy capital and debt load tied to fiber builds and data centers.

Find out about the key risks to this BCE narrative.

Next Steps

Given this mix of concerns and potential upsides, it makes sense to look through the data yourself and not rely only on headlines. To weigh both sides quickly and shape your own view, review the 3 key rewards and 4 important warning signs.

title: BCE is Now Oversold
content:
Legendary investor Warren Buffett advises to be fearful when others are greedy, and be greedy when others are fearful.  One way we can try to measure the level of fear in a given stock is through a technical analysis indicator called the Relative Strength Index, or RSI, which measures momentum on a scale of zero to 100.  A stock is considered to be oversold if the RSI reading falls below 30.

In trading on Tuesday, shares of BCE Inc (Symbol: BCE) entered into oversold territory, hitting an RSI reading of 28.5, after changing hands as low as $23.8039 per share.  By comparison, the current RSI reading of the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) is 46.9.  A bullish investor could look at BCE's 28.5 RSI reading today as a sign that the recent heavy selling is in the process of exhausting itself, and begin to look for entry point opportunities on the buy side.    The chart below shows the one year performance of BCE shares:

Looking at the chart above, BCE's low point in its 52 week range is $20.28 per share, with $26.52 as the 52 week high point — that compares with a last trade of $23.83. Free Report: Top 8%+ Dividends (paid monthly)

title: BCE AI Bet Puts Sovereign Data Centers At Heart Of Investment Story
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

BCE (TSX:BCE) is investing in a large scale AI data center in Canada focused on high performance computing. The company is expanding its partnership with BUZZ HPC to deliver sovereign AI infrastructure across Canadian regions. The initiative is aimed at Canadian enterprises and public sector clients that require high capacity AI computing and domestic data residency.

BCE, best known for its telecom and media operations, is putting more focus on AI infrastructure as a separate business line. The partnership with BUZZ HPC gives BCE access to high performance computing expertise while keeping the physical infrastructure rooted in Canada. For investors, this sits at the intersection of digital infrastructure, cloud style services and AI workloads.

The scale of BCE's AI data center plans and the sovereign focus may influence how Canadian banks, insurers, governments and industrials approach their AI rollouts over time. Readers may want to watch how quickly BCE attracts anchor clients, how it prices capacity and how management frames this AI initiative alongside its traditional network and media assets.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for BCE by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on BCE.TSX:BCE Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Apr 2026

3 things going right for BCE that this headline doesn't cover.

This AI data center build out pushes BCE further into enterprise computing, where it will compete more directly with players like Rogers, Telus and hyperscale cloud providers. The Merritt facility, combined with the planned 300 megawatt site in Saskatchewan, signals an attempt to turn Bell AI Fabric into a core part of the business model rather than a side offering. For you as an investor, the key question is whether these long-duration, capital-heavy projects help diversify away from pressured wireless service revenues and legacy media, or simply add another layer of execution and balance sheet risk.

How This Fits Into The BCE Narrative

The expansion with BUZZ HPC and the focus on high density GPU infrastructure supports the idea that AI powered enterprise solutions can become a higher margin, diversified revenue stream alongside BCE’s core connectivity services. The very high capital intensity of AI data centers could challenge the narrative that BCE can ease CapEx and focus on debt reduction and dividend flexibility, especially with sector price competition weighing on telecom earnings. The push into sovereign AI infrastructure and large scale data centers is only partially reflected in earlier narratives focused on fiber and consumer ARPU, so the long term role of Bell AI Fabric in overall profitability may not be fully captured yet.

Story Continues

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story.  Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for BCE to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

⚠️ Large AI focused projects increase capital needs at a time when analysts already flag debt coverage and earnings pressure as key risks for BCE. ⚠️ Competition from other Canadian telcos and global cloud providers could limit pricing power for AI compute capacity and slow the ramp up of utilization. 🎁 If Bell AI Fabric gains traction with regulated sectors that need domestic data residency, BCE could build a differentiated position in sovereign AI infrastructure. 🎁 The BUZZ HPC partnership brings GPU and liquid cooling expertise that may allow BCE to add enterprise value on top of its existing fiber and data center footprint.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on how quickly the Merritt facility and the planned Saskatchewan data center move from build out to contracted capacity, and whether BCE discloses meaningful AI related revenue contributions. Watch for clarity on funding, including use of the renewed Medium Term Notes program, and how management balances these investments with free cash flow goals after the 2025 dividend cut. Any updates on pricing, contract length and client mix, especially across banks, governments and industrials, will help you judge whether BCE is building a durable AI infrastructure franchise or simply adding another capital intensive segment to an already busy portfolio.

title: Bell Canada renews Medium Term Notes (MTN) program
content:
MONTRÉAL, April 6, 2026 /CNW/ - Bell Canada (Bell) today announced the filing of a prospectus supplement to a short form base shelf prospectus dated April 2, 2026 with the various securities regulatory authorities in all provinces of Canada to renew Bell's MTN program.

The MTN program will enable Bell to offer MTN Debentures from time to time until May 2, 2029. The MTN Debentures will be fully and unconditionally guaranteed by BCE Inc. (TSX: BCE) (NYSE: BCE). Consistent with past practice, the MTN program was renewed to continue to provide Bell with financial flexibility and efficient access to the Canadian capital markets.

Bell also entered into a dealer agreement under which certain dealers have agreed to act as agents with respect to future offerings of the MTN Debentures.

This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy any securities, nor shall there be any sale of these securities, in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such jurisdiction. Any offer of securities covered by the prospectus supplement will be made by a pricing supplement containing specific information about the terms of any such offering. The MTN Debentures have not been, and will not be, registered under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended (U.S. Securities Act), or any U.S. state securities laws and may not be offered or sold in the United States or to or for the account or benefit of U.S. persons (as defined in Regulation S under the U.S. Securities Act).

About Bell

Bell is Canada's largest communications company,1 leading the way in advanced fibre and wireless networks, enterprise services and digital media. By delivering next-generation technology that leverages cloud-based and AI-driven solutions, we're keeping customers connected, informed and entertained while enabling businesses to compete on the world stage. To learn more, please visit Bell.ca or BCE.ca.

1 Based on total revenue and total combined customer connections.

Media inquiries: Ellen Murphy
media@bell.ca

Investor inquiries: Krishna Somers
krishna.somers@bell.caCision

title: TD Securities Turns Cautious on BCE, Lowers ARPU Estimates
content:
BCE Inc. (NYSE:BCE) is included among the 15 Cheapest Stocks with Highest Dividends.TD Securities Turns Cautious on BCE, Lowers ARPU Estimates

On April 2, TD Securities downgraded BCE Inc. (NYSE:BCE) to Hold from Buy and set a C$37 price target. Analyst Vince Valentini reduced his average revenue per user (ARPU) growth estimates by 100 basis points across the next seven quarters for three major telecom players.

On March 16, Reuters reported that BCE plans to invest an additional $1.7 billion to build a 300-megawatt AI data center in Saskatchewan, with Cerebras Systems and CoreWeave signed on as tenants. A unit of BCE, Bell Canada, is working with a provincial government to develop and operate what it described as the largest purpose-built AI data center in Canada once completed. The company expects to spend around $1.3 billion in capital expenditures in 2026 to support construction. Funding will come from a mix of debt and available cash.

Cerebras Systems is expected to supply AI chips for large-scale training and computing. CoreWeave will provide AI computing capacity using processors from NVIDIA. Construction is scheduled to begin in the spring. The project will roll out in phases, with the first stage expected to be operational in the first half of 2027. The facility is intended to serve as a regional hub for advanced computing. It is expected to support the expansion of Canada’s AI ecosystem and contribute to economic growth in Saskatchewan.

It will also connect to Bell’s fibre network through a partnership with SaskTel. Both companies will act as go-to-market partners to deliver AI-powered products and services. BCE has also raised its forecast for AI-driven solutions revenue to about $2 billion by 2028, up from a prior estimate of roughly $1.5 billion.

BCE Inc. (NYSE:BCE) is a Canada-based communications company. It provides broadband Internet, wireless, television, media, and business communication services through its Bell brand. The company operates through two segments: Bell Communication and Technology Services and Bell Media.

While we acknowledge the potential of BCE as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk.
